source.definition: A garment worn beneath outer trousers to cover the intimate area.

explanation.sourceText: The English word 'underpants' is a compound of 'under' and 'pants', where 'pants' derives from the Middle English 'pantaloon', originally from Italian 'pantaloni', referring to leg coverings.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „underpants” este compus din „under” (sub) și „pants” (pantaloni), unde „pants” provine din engleza mijlocie „pantaloon”, inițial din italianul „pantaloni”, referindu-se la îmbrăcăminte pentru picioare.

source.additional: Plural form; singular is 'underpant' though rarely used.

target.definition: Un articol de îmbrăcăminte purtat sub pantalon pentru a acoperi zona intimă.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'chiloți' comes from the Italian 'calzoni', meaning 'trousers', and was adopted into Romanian through French.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „chiloți” provine din italianul 'calzoni' (pantaloni), preluat prin franceză în limba română.

examples.sourceText: The underpants are made of cotton.
Underpants are an important part of clothing.
examples.targetText: Chiloții sunt o parte importantă a îmbrăcămintei.

learningTips.text: Remember 'chiloți' sounds a bit like 'chili' with an 'o' and 't' at the end—imagine your underpants are as spicy as chili!
The word 'chiloți' comes from Italian 'calzoni', meaning trousers—both relate to legwear, so think of 'chiloți' as leg coverings under your pants.
Note that 'chiloți' is plural; the singular is 'chilot'. Think of the English word 'chili' and add 'o' and 't' to form the Romanian word for underpants.
Imagine putting on your underpants and saying, 'I'm putting on my chili—my chiloți!' to help remember the word.
